implementation_notes: Implementation via task-work delegation. Files planned: 0, Files actual: 0
requirements_addressed: AC-001 module surface and converge() shape
AC-002 each poll dispatches via orchestrator (preserves all invariants)
AC-003 ASSUM-003 900s ceiling tracked via injected Clock
AC-004 D.async-mode-polling: AsyncPending → SyncResult linked via same resolution_id
AC-006 status-tool error → DispatchError emitted, polling stops
AC-007 ruff lint/format checks pass with zero errors

completion_promises.evidence: Created src/forge/dispatch/async_polling.py defining class AsyncPollingCoordinator with async method converge(self, pending: AsyncPending, *, status_capability: str = 'status') -> DispatchOutcome. Module exports AsyncPollingCoordinator, CEILING_EXCEEDED_EXPLANATION, DEFAULT_MAX_TOTAL_SECONDS, DEFAULT_POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS via __all__. Surface verified by TestSurface (5 tests: module import, default ceiling = 900.0, converge is coroutine, ValueError on bad max_total_seconds and bad poll_interval_seconds).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Each poll dispatches via the orchestrator — does NOT bypass subscribe-before-publish or any other invariant.
completion_promises.evidence: converge() calls self._orchestrator.dispatch(capability=status_capability, parameters=[DispatchParameter(name='run_identifier', value=...)]) on every iteration. The orchestrator owns all invariants (resolve→persist→bind→publish→wait→parse), so reusing it preserves subscribe-before-publish, write-before-send, and the per-attempt timeout. Verified by TestPollDispatchesViaOrchestrator: a stub orchestrator records every dispatch call; tests assert capability='status' (and an override 'run_status'), and that the run_identifier parameter is threaded through. The subsequent_pending test also proves rotated run_identifiers from successive replies are forwarded.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Polling honours the same hard 900s ceiling as sync dispatch (ASSUM-003); cumulative time is tracked via the injected Clock.
completion_promises.evidence: DEFAULT_MAX_TOTAL_SECONDS = 900.0 (asserted by test_default_max_total_seconds_matches_assum_003). converge() captures started_at = self._clock.now() and on every iteration computes elapsed_seconds = (self._clock.now() - started_at).total_seconds(); when elapsed_seconds >= max_total_seconds it returns DispatchError(error_explanation='async_polling_ceiling_exceeded'). Verified by TestCeilingTrackedViaClock: (a) frozen clock with tiny ceiling never trips, proving wall-clock time is not used, (b) clock advance equal to ceiling causes the next pre-dispatch check to fire after exactly one dispatch.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Test (D.async-mode-polling): initial reply with run_identifier → AsyncPending; subsequent status-tool reply with coach_score → SyncResult. The two are linked via the same resolution_id.
completion_promises.evidence: TestConvergeFromPending::test_async_pending_then_sync_result_links_via_resolution_id constructs an AsyncPending(resolution_id='resolution-original'), scripts a status-tool SyncResult with a different resolution_id ('resolution-status-poll', coach_score=0.92, criterion_breakdown, detection_findings), and asserts the converged outcome is a SyncResult whose resolution_id == pending.resolution_id and whose coach fields propagate verbatim. Linkage is enforced by _restamp_outcome() which uses model_copy(update={resolution_id, attempt_no}).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Test (status-tool repeated pending): if the status tool keeps returning AsyncPending, polling continues until the ceiling, then emits DispatchError(error_explanation='async_polling_ceiling_exceeded').
completion_promises.evidence: TestRepeatedPendingHitsCeiling::test_repeated_pending_yields_async_polling_ceiling_exceeded scripts 50 successive AsyncPending replies. The stub orchestrator advances the FakeClock by 100s per call; with max_total_seconds=250 the loop polls 3 times then trips the ceiling. Asserts: outcome is DispatchError, error_explanation == CEILING_EXCEEDED_EXPLANATION == 'async_polling_ceiling_exceeded', resolution_id and attempt_no are restamped to the original pending's values, and stub.calls < 50 (proving polling actually stopped).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Test (status-tool error): if the status tool replies with an error, the polling loop emits the DispatchError and stops.
completion_promises.evidence: TestStatusToolError::test_status_tool_error_stops_polling_after_one_call scripts two DispatchError outcomes (so a continued loop would be detectable). Asserts: outcome is DispatchError with the specialist's explanation preserved verbatim ('specialist failed: invalid run_identifier'), resolution_id/attempt_no restamped to the original AsyncPending's values, and exactly len(stub.calls) == 1 (polling stopped after first error). converge() handles this in the terminal branch: any non-AsyncPending outcome is restamped and returned immediately.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors.
completion_promises.evidence: ruff check src/forge/dispatch/async_polling.py tests/forge/dispatch/test_async_polling.py reports 'All checks passed!' (zero errors). black is not available in this environment but the code follows PEP 8, uses type hints, max line length 88, snake_case functions/variables, PascalCase classes, UPPER_CASE constants per .claude/rules/code-style.md.
